witnesses.description: A rational point on the unit circle with its tangent line.
A vertical chord of the radius-five circle with midpoint perpendicular to the radius.
The horizontal diameter line intersects the unit circle at two rational points.

checks.claim: The point (3/5,4/5) lies on the unit circle centered at the origin.
The tangent line at (3/5,4/5) is perpendicular to the radius and contains the point.
checks.notes: The validator checks the tangent line value, radius vector, and dot product with the tangent direction.

checks.claim: The radius through the midpoint of the chord from (3,4) to (3,-4) is perpendicular to the chord.
checks.notes: The validator checks both endpoints lie on x^2+y^2=25, recomputes the midpoint, and checks a zero dot product.

checks.claim: The line y=0 intersects the unit circle at (-1,0) and (1,0).
checks.notes: The validator checks both endpoints lie on y=0 and x^2+y^2=1, recomputes their midpoint, and identifies the right intersection.

checks.claim: The point (1,1) lies on the unit circle centered at the origin.

data.certificate: The resource regression parses the source SMT-LIB artifact, emits UnsatFarkas evidence, and independently checks the certificate.

checks.notes: Exact coordinate replay computes squared radius 2 for (1,1), while the malformed row claims squared radius 1; the source QF_LRA artifact isolates that final equality conflict for Farkas checking.

checks.claim: The right intersection of y=0 with the unit circle has x-coordinate 2.

data.certificate: The resource regression parses the source SMT-LIB artifact, emits UnsatFarkas evidence, and independently checks the certificate.

checks.notes: Exact circle-line replay computes the right intersection as (1,0), while the malformed row claims x-coordinate 2; the source QF_LRA artifact isolates that final equality conflict for Farkas checking.

checks.claim: General Euclidean circle geometry, tangent theorems, power-of-a-point, and inversion require future Lean reconstruction.

data.target_theorem_shape: Coordinate-free circle theorems including tangent-radius perpendicularity, chord perpendicular bisectors, power of a point, cyclic quadrilaterals, and inversion
data.future_checker: Lean module with no sorryAx dependencies plus exact coordinate certificate bridges for finite circle witnesses

checks.notes: The finite rows check fixed rational coordinate witnesses; general Euclidean circle theorems remain proof-assistant work.
